texto: There's relief in not having to be outside. No gardening, no mowing the lawn, no tyranny of long daylight hours to fill with productive activity. We rip through summer, burning the hours and tearing up the land. Then snow comes like a bandage, and winter heals the wounds.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jerry Dennis: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “There's relief in not having to be outside. No gardening, no mowing the lawn, no tyranny of long …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, farming, michigan pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de michigan e snow.

frase_original: There's relief in not having to be outside. No gardening, no mowing the lawn, no tyranny of long daylight hours to fill with productive activity. We rip through summer, burning the hours and tearing up the land. Then snow comes like a bandage, and winter heals the wounds.
